human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for university-analytics-specific boundaries, metric-definition rigor, source reconciliation, learner and study record handling, privacy and consent caveats, academic judgment and ethics escalation, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to university analytics, metric definition, source lineage, reconciliation notes, caveat design, disclosure-risk escalation, and higher-education records rather than generic BI work.

Authority sources cover FERPA, Department of Education privacy guidance, Title IV recordkeeping and disclosure rules, FSA handbook record guidance, IPEDS, IPEDS Glossary, College Scorecard documentation, CIP, CEDS, accreditation guidance, Common Rule, OHRP consent guidance, Uniform Guidance, and SEC KPI disclosure guidance.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope analysis, metric ambiguity, stale and conflicting data, unsupported causal and disclosure claims, program and study workflow handling, academic judgment and ethics escalation, grading boundary refusal, consent and official evaluation blockers, missing facts, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/higher-education-and-research-institutions/university-analytics-specialist/ so it remains distinct from registrar, faculty review, accreditation, grants, research compliance, privacy, legal, academic program administration, and faculty support lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated university analytics specialist for metric definition, higher-education reporting, source reconciliation, learner and study record caveat design, and safe escalation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
